scope: Durable persistence for secret-free profile-bound Omnigent OAuth host bindings and leases

gapStatus.notes: Direct Codex, Omnigent, and OAuth maintenance consumers are not yet wired through one release-last ProviderProfileManager lease.

gapStatus.requirement: Profile-bound host persistence, materialization, preflight, and routing
gapStatus.notes: Added durable binding and host-lease tables linked to Provider Profiles, with one binding and one host lease per profile, unique provider-lease ownership, credential generation, lifecycle timestamps, expiry indexing, and cascade cleanup. Runtime materialization, exact-host authentication preflight, automatic routing, and bridge consumption remain open.

gapStatus.requirement: Generation drain, cleanup, recovery, diagnostics, and checkpoints
gapStatus.notes: Durable generation, status, heartbeat, expiry, host/session, and bridge identifiers now exist for later reconciliation. Generation advancement, drain transitions, ordered cleanup, reconciliation activities, checkpoint rules, and Workflow Detail projections remain open.

Added database metadata contract tests for profile and provider-lease exclusivity and expiry indexing. Pytest remains unavailable, so the focused unit suite did not start; equivalent direct metadata smoke, compilation, migration graph, and diff hygiene passed. Lifecycle boundary and hermetic integration coverage remain open.

targetedLocalChecks.command: Direct invocation of both persistence metadata contract tests

summary: Attempt 5 partially closes the durable profile-bound host gap by persisting the secret-free contracts introduced in attempt 4. The remaining MM-1207 runtime lifecycle, materialization, routing, recovery, diagnostics, and boundary-test backlog remains open and bounded by the verifier report.
